name: Fruited Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
Crust:
1 1/4 cups vanilla wafers, crushed
Topping:
2 cups assorted fresh fruits (such as strawberries, sliced kiwi, blueberries, raspberries, or blackberries)
1/3 cup red currant jelly
1 tablespoon brandy (optional)
Grease the sides of an 8-inch or 9-inch springform pan lightly.
Combine the crust ingredients and mix well. Press evenly over the bottom of the pan. Chill while preparing the filling.
In a bowl fitted with an electric mixer, combine cream cheese, sugar, half-and-half, flour, vanilla extract, and salt. Blend until smooth.
Add eggs one at a time, blending until smooth.
Pour batter over the crust.
Bake at 350°F for 1 hour or until the center is just set. Turn off the oven; let cool in the oven with the door open for 30 minutes.
Remove to a wire rack; loosen from the rim of the pan. Cool completely and chill for 4 hours.
Topping may be prepared up to 3 hours ahead.
Arrange fruit in concentric circles attractively over the surface of the cake.
Heat jelly with brandy until melted. Brush over the fruit and let chill for 30 minutes to set the glaze.
Makes 8 servings.
Nutrition per serving: Calories 392, Protein 19g, Carbohydrates 51g, Fat 14g, Cholesterol 102mg, Sodium 303mg, Calcium 193mg
